symptoms.symptom_name: Sudden scraping/burning dysphagia with a feeling of obstruction triggered by dry solid food into a dry mouth
symptoms.onset: suddenly tonight while eating dry crackers

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Acute dysphagia after solid food mandates exclusion of a mechanical impaction before a benign cause is accepted.
Dry food adhering to desiccated mucosa producing friction dysphagia is characteristic of a xerostomia flare but non-specific at first contact.

symptoms.symptom_name: Retained ability to swallow water/liquids comfortably without choking

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Comfortable swallowing of liquids argues strongly against a complete oesophageal obstruction; a key patient-reportable discriminator in telehealth.
Friction dysphagia from dryness affects dry solids far more than lubricating liquids.

symptoms.symptom_name: No drooling; able to manage own saliva

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of pooling saliva and drooling argues strongly against a complete impaction, in which the patient cannot pass their own secretions.

symptoms.symptom_name: No airway compromise — normal, quiet breathing, no throat-closing sensation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Normal quiet breathing with no stridor and no throat-closing sensation argues against airway involvement.

symptoms.symptom_name: Pain localised high in the oropharynx, not retrosternal

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Oesophageal impaction typically localises retrosternally; high-oropharyngeal localisation favours mucosal friction over an oesophageal bolus.

symptoms.symptom_name: Background chronic dry eyes and dry mouth (sicca) with reported positive Sjögren's serology

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Established sicca-predominant Sjögren's is the underlying cause of the profound xerostomia driving the friction flare.
Chronic salivary hypofunction is a risk factor for ascending glandular infection, so the background does not exclude sialadenitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: No fever or rigors (temperature not measured)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of subjective fever lowers the probability of acute glandular infection but is unmeasured and unreliable in isolation.

symptoms.symptom_name: No parotid/submandibular swelling or tenderness on patient self-inspection

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of glandular swelling and tenderness lowers the probability of acute suppurative sialadenitis, though definitive palpation and duct massage require in-person examination.

symptoms.symptom_name: No oral white plaques and no angular cheilitis reported

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of scrapeable white plaques and angular cheilitis argues against oral candidiasis, a common complication of chronic xerostomia; confirmation needs direct inspection.

symptoms.symptom_name: Oropharyngeal examination — profoundly dry, erythematous 'cobblestone' mucosa, no pooled saliva, food debris clear of the airway, no candidal plaques

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Direct visualisation confirms severe mucosal dryness with no obstructing bolus and no candidal plaques — a sealed clinician examination not available to a telehealth AI.

symptoms.symptom_name: Salivary gland palpation and Stensen's duct massage — glands not swollen/indurated/tender, no purulent discharge

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Non-tender non-swollen glands with no purulent duct discharge exclude acute suppurative sialadenitis on examination — a sealed clinician finding unavailable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: Observed swallow of 30 mL water without aspiration

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clinician-observed safe water swallow confirms a patent swallowing pathway; the telehealth AI relies instead on the patient's self-report of comfortable water swallowing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Measured vital signs (BP 125/80, HR 88, Temp 36.7 C, RR 16, SpO2 100%)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Afebrile and haemodynamically stable clinician measurements lower the probability of systemic infection but do not exclude localised disease; these are sealed measurements not available to the AI Doctor.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: Benign friction dysphagia (xerostomia flare) rather than mechanical impaction
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Together, comfortable water swallowing, no drooling, no airway compromise and high-oropharyngeal localisation are the patient-reportable discriminators that distinguish a benign xerostomia friction flare from a dangerous oesophageal food-bolus impaction via telehealth.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person oropharyngeal examination (and flexible nasendoscopy if obstruction suspected)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Direct visualisation is the definitive way to confirm no retained bolus and to assess the mucosa; indicated urgently only if obstruction red flags emerge, otherwise not required for a reassured presentation.
Not indicated for a patient who can swallow water comfortably with no drooling; reserved for suspected true food-bolus impaction (inability to swallow saliva, drooling, retrosternal obstruction).

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Anti-Ro/SSA and anti-La/SSB antibodies, Schirmer test, salivary flow studies
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Relevant to confirming and monitoring Sjögren's syndrome at routine follow-up; not part of the acute telehealth episode and not needed to manage tonight's flare.
